pains.summary: Bizible caused division between teams due to lack of understanding about attribution
pains.quote: I think it's just a lack of understanding about what we're trying to do with attribution, right? So sometimes we have sales calls, so we have a sales ops team and they've been trying to create goals for attribution saying that the sales team is gonna, you know, be driving X percent, you know, of pipeline using whatever attribution model and market marketing's gonna be driving another percentage. And I think there's a confusion sometimes because, you know, obviously you're sales or you're maybe a partnership, you're a strategic alliances team and you're not hitting the goal, but you don't understand what goes into what, what 30% attribution actually means, right? Because it's really activity based. So I think that's where it's been a little problematic
pains.speaker: Bobby Mehta

pains.summary: Attribution used to divide rather than unify teams
pains.quote: the way we leverage attribution, even at, at a company has been more to divide, rather to unify or make optimization decisions
pains.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T) (He/Him)

pains.summary: Manual custom Tableau reporting for campaign and channel attribution was time-consuming
pains.quote: we've basically been able to leverage visible touch points and stitch together semblance of channel and campaign attribution using Tableau to visualize and, and get those standard reporting out. But it's pretty manual and custom
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Difficulty forecasting pipeline based on marketing activities
pains.quote: what is hard for us right now is the predictability, pre predictability of forecasting a pipeline based on our marketing activities and other activities that are ha are happening at an account level
pains.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

pains.summary: Running two systems in parallel is costly
pains.quote: running two systems in parallel is costly for us
pains.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

pains.summary: Security and legal reviews take significant time for new vendor onboarding
pains.quote: security is gonna take its time
pains.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

pains.summary: Need raw data outside platform for custom analytics and ad hoc requests
pains.quote: we still want that ability to reference the touchpoint data in Salesforce, because we do leverage just direct Salesforce reports quite often
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Event and webinar spend not consistently tracked in Salesforce, hurting ROI analysis
pains.quote: I think what I did, I had to do an analysis last year and so I asked them to go back in 2024 and populate the spend in a lot of those. So a lot of the 2024 events have it but they're not doing it in a live kind of as we go basis
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Difficulty getting structured answers and building the system has taken a toll on the team with excessive back-and-forth
pains.quote: I've just been hearing feedback from the team and I just want to convey that is that just getting to the end results just involves a lot of back and forth and it's personally has taken a lot of toll on the team to build the system and to build the process. We haven't been able to get structured answers yet.
pains.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

pains.summary: Still in setup mode more than halfway through contract, which is too long for a SaaS product
pains.quote: we are almost more than halfway through our contract for the year and we're still in setup mode, I would say. And having this much time in setup is way too much from a SaaS point of view.
pains.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

pains.summary: Difficulty understanding campaign type vs channel taxonomy and how data is categorized
pains.quote: Just, just on that for instance, but since there are so many nuances and you're learning the system. Is there contextual help as we look at, like, as you were describing what's the difference between campaign type and channel is a contextual help right there itself, or, or we have to go search somewhere else.
pains.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

pains.summary: Challenges with duplicate data from Marketo requiring them to turn off Marketo data feeds
pains.quote: I think what we did, correct me if I'm wrong, but I think we turned off sending any Marketo data over because it was creating duplicates. We used Salesforce email using SFDC campaigns to track emails.
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Need to audit and validate large numbers that seem unexpectedly high, like 654 opportunities progressing to qualification
pains.quote: Yeah, that seems high. Is there a way for us to audit this? We don't have to do it live on this call, but, like, if we wanted to check that these 654 were legitimate or if we needed to tweak anything.
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Difficulty getting to answers without guided help - wouldn't have been able to navigate to quarterly comparison view alone
pains.quote: And so I like this is another like how do I get back to I just click the grid. No, see, it's great. Great data and like I just, it's hard to get to it, you know, we like I don't know that I would have been able to get there on my own without us going through those questions and answers
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Operational challenge of getting spend data into Salesforce for non-ad channels like events and webinars
pains.quote: completing our spend picture, right? Because we don't currently operationally, we're not putting in spend like we should be in Salesforce. So like that's gonna be an operational project we take on.
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Pipeline projection showed 50% increase with only 7 days left in quarter, which seemed unrealistic
pains.quote: i think the projection like had a 50 increase based on what we have right now and there was only seven days left in the quarter so that seemed a little high to us
pains.speaker: Bobby Mehta

pains.summary: Difficulty packaging RevSure data into a story that makes sense for leadership QBRs
pains.quote: I do feel like there's a lot of good data in RevSure for us, how do we package it in a story that makes sense to deliver to our leaders? You know, that's what we run into.
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

pains.summary: Salesforce permissions still missing after multiple sync attempts, blocking pipeline projections
pains.quote: I know we've been going back and forth on Salesforce. The team mentioned to me they tried a full sync again today, but it seems like the permissions are still missing.
pains.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

pains.summary: Pipeline projections were severely inaccurate, showing 126M vs actual 46M in Q4 2025
pains.quote: our projections showed somewhere around 126 million for last quarter, and the actual pipeline was around 46 million, so that was way off
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Pipeline generation declining significantly year over year, especially from SDR/BDR team
pains.quote: if you look at February in particular they generated 100 million and this year only nine oh
pains.speaker: Bobby Mehta

pains.summary: Opportunity values in Salesforce are unreliable due to automatic calculation based on employee count
pains.quote: the way they actually have the initial opportunity value is a number of employees times a fixed price per employee
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: BDR/SDR team underperforming with low activity levels on calls and emails
pains.quote: we have an SDR team that we believe is underperforming. and you know we've taken corrective action to kind of you know get them to a certain threshold of activity every day in terms of calls and emails
pains.speaker: Bobby Mehta

pains.summary: Marketing engagement down 30% year over year
pains.quote: they are down for marketing, they're down by marketing for about 30%
pains.speaker: Bobby Mehta

pains.summary: Difficulty getting accurate spend data into Salesforce for ROI calculations
pains.quote: that's the lift right is getting our spend consistently in salesforce for a lot of days
pains.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

tools_switched_from.reason: Limited flexibility, no recommendation engine, caused team division, stopped innovating
tools_switched_from.quote: I think it's, I think it's fine. I think that it does seem quite limited, you know, when you're talking about attribution, it's not that flexible. Yeah. But there are some attributes that we do like, you know, if you're just thinking about touchpoint it do, it does a great job. Just being able to surface the touchpoints that all our prospects are, you know, engaging with us, being able to report on that's really useful, but it doesn't really have a recommendation engine, which your product does
tools_switched_from.speaker: Bobby Mehta

tools_switched_from.reason: Caused division between teams rather than unifying them
tools_switched_from.quote: the way we leverage attribution, even at, at a company has been more to divide, rather to unify or make optimization decisions. So it was, it's more around that education piece too, is that people start to create division based on attribution
tools_switched_from.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T) (He/Him)

tools_switched_from.quote: they've really stopped innovating on biz and not done anything at all for the last three years
tools_switched_from.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T) (He/Him)

tools_switched_from.reason: Used for custom visualization but required extensive manual work to build reports that RevSure offers out of the box
tools_switched_from.quote: with those are things that we would spend, you know, hours on, you know, trying to build in Tableau. So to see it out of the box, it makes sense
tools_switched_from.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

tools_switched_from.reason: Limited to contact role attribution only, cannot switch between attribution models
tools_switched_from.quote: we're limited and visible we can't really just switch between attribution models right it is contact role and that's what we're stuck with
tools_switched_from.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

tools_switched_from.reason: Using for reporting but wanted integrated solution; still using Tableau with Salesforce data
tools_switched_from.quote: Bobby and Josh, they do reporting in Tableau using Salesforce tables.
tools_switched_from.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

political_tussles.summary: Sales ops created attribution goals that teams didn't understand, leading to finger-pointing when goals were missed
political_tussles.quote: sometimes we have sales calls, so we have a sales ops team and they've been trying to create goals for attribution saying that the sales team is gonna, you know, be driving X percent, you know, of pipeline using whatever attribution model and market marketing's gonna be driving another percentage. And I think there's a confusion sometimes because, you know, obviously you're sales or you're maybe a partnership, you're a strategic alliances team and you're not hitting the goal, but you don't understand what goes into what, what 30% attribution actually means
political_tussles.speaker: Bobby Mehta

political_tussles.tension_label: Attribution used to divide teams rather than optimize
political_tussles.summary: Harjot notes attribution was weaponized to create division rather than drive optimization decisions
political_tussles.quote: the way we leverage attribution, even at, at a company has been more to divide, rather to unify or make optimization decisions. So it was, it's more around that education piece too, is that people start to create division based on attribution
political_tussles.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T) (He/Him)

political_tussles.summary: Marketing leader reports to CRO and faces pressure to show what marketing is doing to generate pipeline next quarter
political_tussles.quote: our growth leader reports directly to the CRO. The past has been reporting to the head of sales is the question always will come is what is marketing doing that will generate pipeline in the next quarter or the two quarters? What does that projection looks like based on active campaigns? I think giving that answer sometimes is hard
political_tussles.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T) (He/Him)

political_tussles.tension_label: pipeline ownership and quarterly vs annual planning
political_tussles.summary: Discussion about whether sales reps change ACV year vs. quarterly pipeline targets, indicating misalignment between marketing's quarterly focus and sales' annual operating model
political_tussles.quote: Yeah, I don't gosh I don't think that really applies to us right because we're on an annual system right so we have pipeline for Well, we will for for this year, so obviously twenty twenty five. And then if if if a rep is talking to an account and they're like, hey, we can't make a decision this year, we're going to kick it to next year. All the PD, the rep is going to just change that. What we call ACV year from twenty twenty five to twenty twenty six.
political_tussles.speaker: Bobby Mehta

political_tussles.tension_label: attribution scope - where marketing responsibility ends
political_tussles.summary: Josh clarifies that growth marketing's responsibility ends at pipeline creation, not closed-won, suggesting tension about what marketing should be measured on
political_tussles.quote: it's basically taking linear model but only applying the linear model to all the touch points before the account reaches pipeline stage like that pipeline is our cutoff like that's kind of where our responsibility ends right
political_tussles.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

political_tussles.summary: Debate over whether current quarter pipeline is driven by sales activity vs marketing activity
political_tussles.quote: the current pipeline is more driven by pipeline in the current quarter is driven by more of the sales activity than more of marketing activity. Right? Because the BDR team and our BD, AEs are the ones who are creating pipeline in the current quarter.
political_tussles.speaker: Harjot Singh

political_tussles.summary: Leadership presented with 240M projection that later dropped 100M, damaging trust
political_tussles.quote: we and I think we actually did share this with senior leadership, you know, if we say, hey, you know, we have this platform and, you know, it's showing, you know, we're going to get to 240 or 250, whatever it was at that time. And then, you know, it actually ends up being like 100 million less. you know that that's a big miss
political_tussles.speaker: Bobby Mehta

contrarians.claim: They want to deliberately NOT position RevSure as an AI tool to avoid triggering additional security scrutiny
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors lead with AI messaging; this client wants to downplay AI to speed procurement
contrarians.quote: I am not gonna put this as a AI tool. I'll be very clear. Unless you put something in, in, in from a documentation point of view, I am putting this as a marketing analytics tool
contrarians.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

contrarians.claim: They prefer modular purchase starting with non-attribution modules (predictability) rather than core attribution
contrarians.why_unusual: Most buyers lead with attribution; they lead with pipeline predictability as entry point
contrarians.quote: what we really want as a key thing is the predictability to what is hard for us right now is the predictability, pre predictability of forecasting a pipeline based on our marketing activities
contrarians.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

contrarians.claim: They explicitly do NOT want to use Marketo for email tracking, preferring Salesforce campaigns instead, and turned off Marketo data entirely due to duplicates
contrarians.why_unusual: Most marketing ops teams default to Marketo as source of truth for email engagement; deliberately choosing Salesforce campaign member statuses over native Marketo integration is contrarian
contrarians.quote: I think what we did, correct me if I'm wrong, but I think we turned off sending any Marketo data over because it was creating duplicates. We used Salesforce email using SFDC campaigns to track emails.
contrarians.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

contrarians.claim: They don't use forecast category in Salesforce and forecasting is done top-down rather than bottom-up
contrarians.why_unusual: Most mature SaaS companies use Salesforce forecast categories and bottom-up forecasting; Lyra's manual top-down approach with rep-adjusted 'at risk'/'commit' fields is less common
contrarians.quote: So we do have something called manual forecast category where the rep is basically saying, for example, like if they feel like they're not gonna win it, they do at risk, right? If they do feel like they're gonna win it, they would do something like commit. And that's a manual thing that the rep is changing.
contrarians.speaker: Bobby Mehta

contrarians.claim: They don't want to use out-of-box probability percentage; instead use projected win rate as just a projection/expectation
contrarians.why_unusual: Most Salesforce orgs use standard probability fields; explicitly rejecting OOB probability for a custom 'expectation' field is atypical
contrarians.quote: just like the comment says, like we don't use the out of box probability percentage, but that's more about an expectation. There's another field called projected win rate, which is just that, it's just a projection.
contrarians.speaker: Bobby Mehta

contrarians.claim: They want contact roles PLUS buyer persona criteria, not all contacts on account
contrarians.why_unusual: Standard attribution is either contact-role-only or all-contacts-on-account; wanting a hybrid 'contact roles plus specified personas' is unusual
contrarians.quote: better solution, I think Harjit suggested this too, is take contact roles, but then in addition to that, say buyer persona, like if it's a benefits leader, and that's the field we would use, there's a field called buyer persona, it would say if that equals benefits leader, HR leader, benefits specialist, then they are also eligible for attribution, but it's only those folks. It's not someone who's like an analytics manager. We don't really care about his engagement.
contrarians.speaker: Bobby Mehta

contrarians.claim: They don't look at pipeline quarterly because health plans renew annually, making quarterly pipeline metrics misleading
contrarians.why_unusual: Most B2B SaaS companies obsess over quarterly pipeline; Lyra's annual healthcare enrollment cycle makes quarterly views less relevant
contrarians.quote: they don't look at pipeline in a quarterly sense because pipeline for them doesn't necessarily close quarterly, right? Because health plans run like very yearly. So it's like towards the end of the year where they're like building a pipeline, building a pipeline. and then there's like the jumps right because some plans they like renew in october or they renew in like this it's like october november december january
contrarians.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

contrarians.claim: MMX (marketing mix modeling) is better for budget reallocation than campaign-level attribution when budgets are flat
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors push incremental budget scenarios; Lyra's reality is zero incremental budget, making reallocation the only use case
contrarians.quote: we're at an organization where budgets are kind of locked, right? So it's rare that we find ourselves, actually it doesn't happen, where we have incremental budget to spend. So it would just be in a reallocation exercise, right?
contrarians.speaker: Bobby Mehta

contrarians.claim: Content syndication should potentially be excluded from pipeline modeling despite what data might show
contrarians.why_unusual: Revenue teams usually want all data included; explicitly wanting to exclude a channel based on business judgment is unusual
contrarians.quote: we don't want to take into account content syndication activity as an example do we want to filter that out in our pipeline modeling as an example
contrarians.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

revsure_problems.problem: Cannot easily create custom calculated metrics on the fly for combined funnel stage analysis
revsure_problems.quote: if me or Josh wanted to do like target plus awareness, plus consideration, plus MQA, you know like that come that number combined and then just do cost divided by that number. Yeah, I mean it's kind of an ROI, you know, kind of something like that
revsure_problems.speaker: Bobby Mehta

revsure_problems.problem: Custom Salesforce object writeback not yet available as turnkey managed package
revsure_problems.quote: this is a newer avenue that we're exploring as far as like the development to get it outside of like what we've historically had
revsure_problems.speaker: Jerry Henry

revsure_problems.problem: BigQuery export costs additional $15K/year, not included in base pricing
revsure_problems.quote: it would potentially be around 15k a year to get everything from RevSure into Tableau for you
revsure_problems.speaker: Jerry Henry

revsure_problems.problem: FCA (Funnel Conversion Attribution) configuration requires backend trigger and takes 1-2 hours to run
revsure_problems.quote: the configurations in FCA take an hour or two to run, and then they're available
revsure_problems.speaker: Harry Hawk

revsure_problems.problem: Cannot do double split in cohort progression view (e.g., topic + territory market)
revsure_problems.quote: here there isn't that double split. There's just the split of what we're seeing attribution by right
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: Custom engagement type filters not working together with opportunity pipeline date filter
revsure_problems.quote: it could be some I'll I'll I'll check with the team again, because I had checked with them this morning. But I'll check again about those filters working together and how we can make them work together
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: Confusion about snapshot view vs. cohort view for quarterly reporting
revsure_problems.quote: I was kind of expecting it to be, so like if we change the date filter to previous fiscal quarter, for example, that would show the snapshot of the previous fiscal quarter, but that's not the case
revsure_problems.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

revsure_problems.problem: Confusion about what 'entered final stage' means vs. maximum stage reached in a quarter
revsure_problems.quote: I would kind of expect that this, I get that it's a snapshot now. this is rendered less useful for us, these two filters, right? Because I mean, ultimately what we're looking at is where's their stage now, right? In the snapshot, what stage are they in right now? And that's always gonna be the case, right?
revsure_problems.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

revsure_problems.problem: UI icons are misleading - table view icon looks like it should show a line chart, causing confusion
revsure_problems.quote: See this to me, like it makes me think I'm going to click on see a line chart when I see that. Exactly. Same, same with this, just their minor, it's minor UI things, but it's good to know that's how it's supposed to be read. I would have intuitively thought that.
revsure_problems.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

revsure_problems.problem: The 'snapshot' feature is misnamed - it's actually a scheduled PDF report, not a data snapshot for comparison
revsure_problems.quote: I think the industry term is very different from snapshots. I got confused. So that's probably a piece of feedback to that. I thought snapshot is a data snapshot you take that you can use for comparison. So this is very different.
revsure_problems.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

revsure_problems.problem: Still in setup mode after more than half the contract period
revsure_problems.quote: we are almost more than halfway through our contract for the year and we're still in setup mode, I would say. And having this much time in setup is way too much from a SaaS point of view.
revsure_problems.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

revsure_problems.problem: Difficulty distinguishing campaign type vs channel vs source system and how they hierarchically relate
revsure_problems.quote: Quick question. What's the difference between campaign type and channel? They seem to be similar.
revsure_problems.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

revsure_problems.problem: Pipeline projection showed impossible 50% increase with 7 days left in quarter
revsure_problems.quote: i think the projection like had a 50 increase based on what we have right now and there was only seven days left in the quarter so that seemed a little high to us
revsure_problems.speaker: Bobby Mehta

revsure_problems.problem: Model includes factors like product type and territory that don't make sense for pipeline prediction before opportunity creation
revsure_problems.quote: if you look at factors, it's taking into account factors like product, which are not defined at opportunity level, like opportunities not even created, how do you even take product into consideration at that point?
revsure_problems.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

revsure_problems.problem: Salesforce permissions still missing after multiple full sync attempts, blocking data flow
revsure_problems.quote: The team mentioned to me they tried a full sync again today, but it seems like the permissions are still missing.
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: Client struggling to package RevSure data into leadership-ready QBR stories
revsure_problems.quote: I do feel like there's a lot of good data in RevSure for us, how do we package it in a story that makes sense to deliver to our leaders?
revsure_problems.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

revsure_problems.problem: Pipeline projection wildly inaccurate (126M vs 46M actual) due to outlier quarter not being detected
revsure_problems.quote: our projections showed somewhere around 126 million for last quarter, and the actual pipeline was around 46 million, so that was way off
revsure_problems.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

revsure_problems.problem: Model too slow to correct downward when velocity slows mid-quarter
revsure_problems.quote: it corrected but it took a long time for it to correct
revsure_problems.speaker: Bobby Mehta

revsure_problems.problem: Amazon deal value showed as $142M in RevSure when actual was $11.6M due to using initial opportunity value not latest
revsure_problems.quote: when i look at in salesforce it's 11.6 million and you guys have like a hundred if you go back to that view we were just looking at it yeah uh
revsure_problems.speaker: Bobby Mehta

revsure_problems.problem: Data sync delays causing stale opportunity values in RevSure
revsure_problems.quote: How often is that supposed to happen? We are having some issues
revsure_problems.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

revsure_problems.problem: Client confused about difference between campaign reallocation (within channel) and MMX (across channels)
revsure_problems.quote: yeah but that then that in that case like would you not rely on campaign reallocation rather than looking at an mmx model
revsure_problems.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

revsure_problems.problem: Feature toggle for using latest opportunity value vs initial value not properly configured initially
revsure_problems.quote: there is a feature toggle that controls which value you see whether you see the first one at the time of pipeline creation or we use the latest value
revsure_problems.speaker: Jayaram Thiyagarajan

best_about_revsure.summary: Out-of-box functionality saves hours of Tableau custom reporting
best_about_revsure.quote: with those are things that we would spend, you know, hours on, you know, trying to build in Tableau. So to see it out of the box, it makes sense
best_about_revsure.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

best_about_revsure.summary: Complete traceability to opportunity/account level for validation
best_about_revsure.quote: Our perspective is you should be able to, traceability is very important to the most grand level. So if I say that this campaign generated this much pipeline or from an attribution perspective, you should be able to look at that pipeline, right? And validate at that opportunity level or at that account level what the, what opportunities and where that, where that particular campaign touched a particular opportunity or not
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

best_about_revsure.summary: Flexibility to switch between lead-based and account-based funnels
best_about_revsure.quote: very easily we are able to switch between perspectives, have an account based funnel, lead based funnel account, you might have a lead based funnel, but you might might want to have an account perspective
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

best_about_revsure.summary: Transparency of data foundation vs. black-box approach
best_about_revsure.quote: The reason being that, you know, you need to have trust on the data itself, right? What data and what coverage we are taking care of, right? So for example, here I've harmonized all of the data. This is the SHO data model, leads, accounts, activities, campaigns, campaign members, et cetera. But it's a fusion data model
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

best_about_revsure.summary: Team impressed with RevSure's intelligence and capabilities
best_about_revsure.quote: You guys are in the top 5% of the smartest marketing team we have run into. And that's exactly who we wanna work with because the only people that are gonna know how to really leverage rev share to the peak is gonna be, well your team is definitely in our top
best_about_revsure.speaker: E.T Mac

best_about_revsure.summary: Pipeline predictability answers CRO's key question about future pipeline
best_about_revsure.quote: it does actually, and it's, it's too good to please for sure that you can get all that out of box and give those answers. So yeah. But this is really helpful and, and exactly what we are looking for
best_about_revsure.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T) (He/Him)

best_about_revsure.summary: Appreciation for Francisco's patience and guidance through repeated questions
best_about_revsure.quote: Thank you for all the explanations and answering questions we probably asked more than once. You've been very patient and I appreciate both of your guidance.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

best_about_revsure.summary: Excitement about Google ad group level data for digital team optimization
best_about_revsure.quote: That's awesome Our digital team will love this
best_about_revsure.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

best_about_revsure.summary: Praise for journey visualization power for win analysis
best_about_revsure.quote: This is powerful, right? Because you could do a win analysis, right? You could look at all the opportunities we've won and try to see if there's any commonalities for journeys. So yeah, this is cool.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Bobby Mehta

best_about_revsure.summary: Appreciation for video enablement materials being super clear
best_about_revsure.quote: Yeah, it was very helpful and super clear. Thank you for that.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

best_about_revsure.summary: Recognition that account progression reporting is in good shape to answer business questions
best_about_revsure.quote: So I feel like we're in a pretty good place where we can answer a lot of those account progression questions we wanted to.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

best_about_revsure.summary: Appreciation for Francisco's quick work adding fields and API names to shared document
best_about_revsure.quote: Great, look at that, super fast.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Bobby Mehta

best_about_revsure.summary: Praise for Bobby's well-documented statistics and field definitions
best_about_revsure.quote: There's a lot of great statistics very very Well written out Bobby great job.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

best_about_revsure.summary: Appreciation for RevSure team's transparency and willingness to explain methodology

immediate.win: Pipeline predictability module showing day-by-day quarter projections
immediate.quote: you are in day 39 of the quarter, and what we are predicting is by the end of this quarter marketing, and you could kind of filter right to various aspects by region, channel, industry, opportunity source, et cetera, how much pipeline you'll end up generating by the end of the quarter
immediate.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

immediate.win: Campaign reallocation recommendations identifying unproductive spend
immediate.quote: this reallocation module automatically identifies the campaigns and about $200,000 of on productive spend, right? That went into events that really didn't generate any pipeline value and reallocating that to the top performing events
immediate.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

immediate.win: Account-level journey stitching and touchpoint aggregation
immediate.quote: for each of these accounts, right? I also have, let's say, some of the more active accounts here, you know, I have also the journey for this account, right? All of the touches that are happening, and you could select, okay, which contact name, you know, all of these contacts within the account, so I've aggregated at the account level
immediate.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

immediate.win: LinkedIn organic post performance tracking (impressions, clicks, reactions, comments)
immediate.quote: with RepShirt, we're also able to to pull in like your LinkedIn posts, like your organic LinkedIn posts, and show you like the click scenes, impressions, suspense, and the comments
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.quote: we've developed these dimensions, so we're able to see the funnel stage impact of a UTM term, a UTM campaign, a UTM source, a UTM content, and the UTM medium
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Marketo email data now included in account engagement trends alongside LinkedIn
immediate.quote: One of the things that we just rolled out and this is something I've been advocating for is here We get the account engagement trend. So right we just pulled this directly from LinkedIn, right like the account engagements, but The exciting thing now is, is that now we're populating Marketo data. So you'll see the Marketo emails open, Marketo emails open rate, and Marketo emails set.
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Custom tofu attribution filter working for pipeline creation analysis
immediate.quote: click on yeah so we're looking at all the engagements before right so we're looking at all the engagements leading up to opportunity pipeline date before opportunity pipeline date that
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Set up pipeline projection views with custom filters for Lyra's business
immediate.quote: I actually set up some views with some filters that are most applicable to our business. You'll see those in our instance, Francisco and Jerry, just so you know, like in parentheses, it has J H and some of the saved views.
immediate.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

immediate.win: Velocity-based adjustment mechanism implemented to catch slowing pipeline generation mid-quarter
immediate.quote: we've incorporated now a velocity based adjustment mechanism that detects when pipeline generation is slowing and adjust projections in real time rather than staying flat
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Updated filters and funnel changes per Bobby's request now live in platform
immediate.quote: all the filters and the funnel changes that Bobby had proposed are in the platform
immediate.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

long_term.win: Full platform adoption with all modules after proving value
long_term.quote: Next year could be a different conversation when we have sort of vetted out things and, and really figured everything out. And then, then it's sort of a replace strategy and it's a different conversation
long_term.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

long_term.win: Salesforce managed package for touchpoint data writeback
long_term.quote: Our team was just all off yesterday as well. So I can follow up on that possibility on what we could write back, which then to your point, then you could have Salesforce into any of those
long_term.speaker: Jerry Henry

long_term.win: Custom attribution models (top of funnel, bottom of funnel, full path)
long_term.quote: it'd be really great, and I don't know if it's feasible. I'd love to hear from you and team to get our custom models in. We have a top of funnel and a bottom of funnel. Top of funnel helps us answer what's contributing to pipeline generation. Bottom of funnel is telling us what's contributing to pipeline acceleration
long_term.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

long_term.win: Pipeline projections with quarterly and annual views to improve forecasting process
long_term.quote: So we can view projections yearly. I'm seeing one of our other customers and the yearly projections as an example. Of course, this is a code, so I can't show it right. But like I was saying, like created year 2025, closing anytime, it'll show you the generated pipeline and the total projection for that. So we can give you the ability to look at projections both from a quarterly and a yearly level. So that's 100% possible with Rapture.
long_term.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

long_term.win: AI-powered natural language querying for non-technical stakeholders
long_term.quote: So yeah, so we have really that's part of our AI component that I can send more info over on that. You can ask certain questions we can maybe if we have time after this to give you like a demo on it, but that's one way where a lot of our customers leverage. It will last. Hey, if we wanted to look into XYZ or what is the influence here? and digest everything in a rupture and then start answering all those for you.
long_term.speaker: Jerry Henry

long_term.win: Enable team-wide adoption with restricted module access and view-only dashboards
long_term.quote: we might want to do is share a specific view with preset filters so that the team doesn't get too lost or confused and have a specific use case that view, you know, solves for our different stakeholders.
long_term.speaker: Josh Holzhaeuser

long_term.win: Get to a state where pipeline projections are trusted enough to present to C-level leadership
long_term.quote: we want to get to a stage and need your help to get to a stage where you can start using these things
long_term.speaker: Harjot (Harr-Joe-T)

long_term.quote: some of these inputs can be taken into consideration while doing velocity, we can add that into the model

long_term.speaker: Harjot Singh

long_term.win: Implement ROI analysis with proper spend attribution across marketing channels
long_term.quote: Josh primarily is working on a ROI project. So just kind of wondering what, obviously, you know, we were planning on using
long_term.speaker: Bobby Mehta

before_state.pain_summary: Manual Tableau reporting stitched from Bizible touchpoints, attribution causing team division rather than optimization, inability to predict pipeline from marketing activities, and no recommendation engine for campaign optimization
kpis_unhealthy: time spent on custom Tableau reporting

after_state.with_revsure: Modular adoption starting with pipeline predictability and demand gen effectiveness, running parallel to Bizible with intent to replace, building custom dashboards for account progression and campaign optimization
wins_realized: pipeline predictability with daily quarter projections
Google ad group level data available
Account progression volume metrics by stage functional
Funnel health targets with % attainment visible
Marketing channel impact dashboard across funnel stages
Pipeline projection views with custom filters
filter/funnel configuration updated per client request
open_issues: Salesforce managed package writeback not yet available
BigQuery export costs extra $15K/year
channel classification still ongoing
custom calculated metrics not yet available
FCA configuration requires backend trigger
double split in cohort views not supported
goal-setting/planning module on roadmap but not available
Need to clean up Salesforce campaign data and UTM tracking
Campaign reallocation spend picture incomplete for non-ad channels
Need validation that custom filters apply to attribution model not just display
UI navigation still difficult without guided help
Data taxonomy rules not yet visible in platform
Salesforce permissions blocking full sync
Model factors need refinement (remove product type, territory)
Daily projection tracking not aligned to long sales cycles
Need to build leadership-ready QBR narratives from raw data
pipeline projection still correcting too slowly for outlier quarters
Q1 2026 projection dropped from 240M to 135M late in quarter
need SDR leading indicators in model
need pre-opportunity stage probability weighting
spend data integration for ROI incomplete
naming conventions don't match Lyra's internal terminology
